debt_collection.name: Debt Collection Notice
debt_collection.governing_law: Provincial Collection and Debt Settlement Services Acts, federal Limitation Periods
key_rights: Right to request verification of the debt in writing
Collectors cannot contact you on Sundays, statutory holidays, or outside 7am–9pm
Limitation period: typically 2 years in Ontario, BC, and Alberta from acknowledgment
Right to complain to provincial consumer protection office — free
Financial Consumer Agency of Canada (FCAC) handles federally regulated creditors
Right to have a credit counselling agency negotiate on your behalf
common_violations: Contacting outside permitted hours or on prohibited days
Attempting to collect a limitation-expired debt without disclosure
Threatening legal action that the collector cannot or does not intend to take
Communicating with employer, family, or third parties without authorization
Using threatening, profane, or intimidating language
debt_collection.forum: Provincial Consumer Protection Office or Small Claims Court
debt_collection.template_response: I write in response to your collection notice dated [DATE]. I request written verification of this debt including the original creditor, full account history, and proof of your licence to collect in this province. I am aware of my rights under the [Province] Collection and Debt Settlement Services Act. Please cease all collection activity until verification is provided. I reserve the right to file a complaint with the provincial consumer protection office.

eviction_notice.name: Eviction / Termination of Tenancy Notice
eviction_notice.governing_law: Provincial Residential Tenancies Acts (Ontario RTA, BC RTA, Alberta RTA, etc.)
key_rights: Ontario: must file with Landlord and Tenant Board (LTB) — cannot self-evict
BC: must apply to Residential Tenancy Branch (RTB)
Right to dispute notice at provincial tribunal
Landlord cannot change locks or remove belongings without tribunal order
Notice periods: non-payment 14 days (ON), cause termination 20 days (ON)
common_violations: Self-help eviction — changing locks without tribunal order (illegal in all provinces)
Harassment or intimidation to force tenant out
Retaliatory notice after tenant complaint to housing authority
Insufficient notice period for the grounds stated
eviction_notice.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [DATE]. I dispute the validity of this notice and intend to file a response with the [LTB/RTB]. I am aware that only a tribunal order can authorize my eviction, and any self-help eviction attempt is illegal. I request all future communications be in writing.

court_summons.name: Court Summons / Statement of Claim
court_summons.governing_law: Provincial Rules of Civil Procedure, Federal Courts Rules
key_rights: 20–30 days to file a Statement of Defence in most provinces
Failure to respond results in default judgment
Small Claims Court for disputes up to $25,000–$35,000 depending on province
Right to legal aid if eligible
Right to challenge improper service
common_violations: Improper service of process
Claim filed in wrong jurisdiction
Limitation period has expired
court_summons.forum: Provincial Superior Court or Small Claims Court
court_summons.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of the Statement of Claim in File No. [NUMBER]. I intend to file a Statement of Defence within the time prescribed. I dispute the claim in its entirety.

employment_termination.name: Termination / Layoff Letter
employment_termination.governing_law: Canada Labour Code (federally regulated), Provincial Employment Standards Acts
key_rights: Minimum notice: 1 week per year of service up to 8 weeks (Ontario ESA)
Severance pay in Ontario: 1 week per year for 5+ years at employer with $2.5M payroll
Right to challenge wrongful termination — no minimum length of service required for human rights claims
Must file employment standards complaint within 2 years of violation
Right to EI (Employment Insurance) — apply immediately after termination
common_violations: Insufficient notice or termination pay under provincial Employment Standards Act
Non-payment of severance where legally required
Termination in violation of Human Rights Code (discriminatory dismissal)
Constructive dismissal without adequate notice
employment_termination.forum: Provincial Employment Standards Branch or Superior Court
employment_termination.template_response: I acknowledge my termination communicated on [DATE]. I request written confirmation of my entitlements including notice pay, severance (if applicable), and vacation pay owed. I note I have [X] years of service and am entitled to minimum notice under the [Provincial] Employment Standards Act. I reserve all rights including the right to file an employment standards complaint.

consumer_notice.name: Consumer / Company Notice
consumer_notice.governing_law: Competition Act, provincial Consumer Protection Acts, CPA Ontario
key_rights: 10-day cooling off period for door-to-door sales and certain contracts
Right to chargeback through credit card for goods/services not received
Right to complain to provincial consumer protection office
Small Claims Court for disputes under $25,000–$35,000
Right to dispute inaccurate information on credit report
common_violations: False or misleading advertising
Unfair practices including unconscionable representation
Not honoring warranty claims
Charging for unauthorized services
consumer_notice.forum: Provincial Consumer Protection Office or Small Claims Court
consumer_notice.template_response: I write to formally dispute [describe issue] under the [Provincial] Consumer Protection Act. I purchased [product/service] on [date]. The [product/service] fails to conform to what was represented. I request [refund/resolution] within 14 days or I will file a complaint with the provincial consumer protection office.
